# DSMS Control Center (`dsms-gui`)

GTK 3 desktop app for **DSMS** — the
[Domain & Storage Management System](https://github.com/DerKellerPotato/dsms).
It edits `/etc/dsms/dsms.conf` through forms, applies the configuration, joins
the domain (Active Directory / Synology / LDAP) and configures home-directory
synchronisation — all without touching the terminal.

The app holds no privileges of its own: every system change is run through
`pkexec` against the **`dsms`** command-line tool, which is a hard dependency.

**Alert: Much of this code was generated by AI. Please do not use it in
production without further testing.**

---

## Install

### From apt / a `.deb`

```bash
sudo apt install dsms-gui      # pulls in the 'dsms' command automatically
```

`dsms-gui` declares `Depends: dsms`, so the command-line tool is installed with
it. Then launch **DSMS Control Center** from the application menu.

### From source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/DerKellerPotato/dsms-gui.git
cd dsms-gui
sudo bash install.sh
```

You also need the CLI installed separately:
[DerKellerPotato/dsms](https://github.com/DerKellerPotato/dsms).

Runtime dependencies: `python3-gi`, `gir1.2-gtk-3.0` (preinstalled on Mint and
Ubuntu desktops), `policykit-1`.

---

## What it does

| Tab | Contents |
|-----|----------|
| **Status** | Output of `dsms status` + a "test user lookup" field |
| **Domain** | Directory type, AD and LDAP connection settings, join account |
| **Home Directory** | Mode (SMB mount / rsync / local), share, shell, groups |
| **Network & System** | Hostname, DNS, hosts, Avahi, NTP, sudo/polkit/hardware groups, login screen |
| **SSH Sync** | Sync server, key, timeouts, hash-based change detection |

**Buttons:** *Save* (`pkexec dsms import`), *Apply to System*
(`pkexec dsms apply`). The **☰** menu has the setup wizard, domain join,
config import/export and **Update from GitHub** (see below).

A first-run **setup wizard** collects the core settings and can install
packages + apply + join in one pass.

See [docs/usage.md](docs/usage.md) for the full walkthrough.

---

## Update from GitHub (debug)

**☰ → Update from GitHub…** downloads the latest `dsms` (CLI) and `dsms-gui`
(this app) straight from GitHub and reinstalls both via `pkexec` — handy for
testing the newest development build without waiting for a package release.
Requires `git`. Restart the app afterwards to load the updated GUI.

The repositories it pulls from are set near the top of `dsms-gui`
(`GITHUB_CLI_REPO`, `GITHUB_GUI_REPO`); the privileged work is done by
`update-from-github.sh`.

---

## Build a `.deb`

```bash
bash packaging/build-deb.sh --version 1.0
# → dist/dsms-gui_1.0_all.deb   (Depends: dsms)
```

## Tests

```bash
bash tests/run-tests.sh
```

Pure-Python tests for the config parse/serialize logic (no GTK/display needed)
plus shell syntax checks.
